hypothesis: A hybrid adaptive batching algorithm that dynamically switches between static-64 and dynamic batching strategies based on a real-time estimate of the current graph size variance within a training epoch will achieve greater overall training speedup than either algorithm alone, because it exploits the superior gradient-update compilation efficiency of static-64 once recompilations are exhausted while retaining dynamic batching's advantage during early training when recompilation overhead dominates, and this benefit will be most pronounced for datasets with heavy-tailed node/edge distributions such as AFLOW where the variance in batch composition is highest.
